ucs.title: [LLM] Evilnum spearphishing LNK chain — wscript/cscript launching JavaScript dropper from KYC-themed archive
ucs.description: Hunts Evilnum's signature initial-access pattern: a spearphishing email with a Google Drive / OneDrive link delivers a ZIP containing LNK shortcuts disguised as PDFs/JPGs (KYC, proof-of-address, trading docs). The LNK invokes wscript.exe or cscript.exe against a .js stager (TerraLoader / More_eggs / PyVil) dropped to %TEMP% / %APPDATA%. This sequence — explorer/archiver parent → wscript|cscript → .js from user-writable path with financial-document lure naming — is highly specific to Evilnum's fintech-targeting tradecraft (ESET 'Evilnum' July 2020; Zscaler PyVil RAT reporting).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="wscript.exe" OR Processes.process_name="cscript.exe") (Processes.process="*.js*" OR Processes.process="*.jse*" OR Processes.process="*.wsf*") (Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Users\\Public\\*") (Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="outlook.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="7zG.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="WinRAR.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="chrome.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="msedge.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="firefox.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=process "(?i)(?<lure>(?:KYC|Proof|POA|POI|Utility|Statement|Invoice|Trading|Compliance|Wire|SWIFT|Passport|ID|Driver|Crypto|Forex|Account)[^\\\\]{0,40}\.(?:js|jse|wsf|lnk|pdf))" | where isnotnull(lure) OR like(process,"%.pdf.js%") OR like(process,"%.jpg.js%") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Evilnum delivery chain — LNK→wscript/cscript→.js from user-writable path with financial-doc lure
let _lure_terms = dynamic(["kyc","poa","poi","proof","utility","statement","invoice","trading","compliance","wire","swift","passport","driver","crypto","forex","account","contract"]);
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
// JavaScript / WScript file invocation — Evilnum stagers are .js, .jse, .wsf
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".js",".jse",".wsf")
// Dropped/clicked from a user-writable archive-extraction location
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_user_writable)
// Spawned from a typical click chain: archive viewer, browser, mail client, or shell
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","outlook.exe","7zg.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe","rar.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe")
| extend CmdLower = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
// Either a financial-doc lure term in cmdline OR the classic double-extension trick
| where CmdLower has_any (_lure_terms)
   or CmdLower matches regex @"\.(pdf|jpg|jpeg|png|doc|docx|xls)\.(js|jse|wsf)"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent      = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child       = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          IsRemote    = InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Evilnum delivery chain — Sentinel SecurityEvent (4688) port
let _lure_terms = dynamic(["kyc","poa","poi","proof","utility","statement","invoice","trading","compliance","wire","swift","passport","driver","crypto","forex","account","contract"]);
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith @"\wscript.exe" or NewProcessName endswith @"\cscript.exe"
| where CommandLine has_any (".js",".jse",".wsf")
| where CommandLine has_any (_user_writable)
| where ParentProcessName endswith @"\explorer.exe"
    or ParentProcessName endswith @"\outlook.exe"
    or ParentProcessName endswith @"\7zG.exe"
    or ParentProcessName endswith @"\7zFM.exe"
    or ParentProcessName endswith @"\WinRAR.exe"
    or ParentProcessName endswith @"\Rar.exe"
    or ParentProcessName endswith @"\chrome.exe"
    or ParentProcessName endswith @"\msedge.exe"
    or ParentProcessName endswith @"\firefox.exe"
| extend CmdLower = tolower(CommandLine)
| where CmdLower has_any (_lure_terms)
    or CmdLower matches regex @"\.(pdf|jpg|jpeg|png|doc|docx|xls)\.(js|jse|wsf)"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          Parent    = ParentProcessName,
          Child     = NewProcessName,
          CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Evilnum LNK-to-JavaScript Spearphishing Stager
id: 8e3b2f4c-1d9a-4a3e-9c7d-evilnum-stager-001
status: experimental
description: Detects Evilnum (G0120) initial-access chain — LNK in a financial-doc-themed archive launches wscript/cscript against a .js/.jse/.wsf stager dropped to a user-writable path.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0120/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2020/07/09/more-evil-deep-look-evilnum-toolset/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.t1204.001
  - attack.t1059.007
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  selection_script:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '.js'
      - '.jse'
      - '.wsf'
  selection_path:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\Downloads\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\7zG.exe'
      - '\7zFM.exe'
      - '\WinRAR.exe'
      - '\Rar.exe'
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
  selection_lure:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'KYC'
      - 'POA'
      - 'Proof'
      - 'Utility'
      - 'Statement'
      - 'Invoice'
      - 'Trading'
      - 'Compliance'
      - 'SWIFT'
      - 'Passport'
      - 'Crypto'
      - 'Forex'
      - '.pdf.js'
      - '.jpg.js'
  condition: selection_image and selection_script and selection_path and selection_parent and selection_lure
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate enterprise JS automation in Temp (rare; baseline before alerting)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Evilnum's almost-exclusive initial access since 2018 has been ZIP-borne LNK files masquerading as KYC / proof-of-address / trading documents sent to forex, crypto, and online-trading firms. The chain explorer/archiver/Outlook → wscript|cscript → .js from %TEMP% with a financial-doc lure name is a tight fingerprint that other JS-using crews (Gootloader, SocGholish) miss because they use different p
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Evilnum AnyDesk / Chrome-credential-theft post-exploitation in fintech estate
ucs.description: Evilnum (G0120) is documented by ESET and Zscaler as deploying AnyDesk (T1219.002) for hands-on-keyboard access on fintech victims, then running NirSoft / ChromePass-class tools to dump browser-stored trading-platform creds (T1555.003) and stealing chrome cookies / session tokens (T1539). This UC fires when an unsigned/unmanaged AnyDesk binary is launched from a non-standard path on the same host where, within 24h, a process reads Chrome's `Login Data` or `Cookies` SQLite store — a chain that almost never occurs benignly.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as anydesk_time values(Processes.process) as anydesk_cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as anydesk_parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="AnyDesk.exe" (Processes.process="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Downloads\\*") by host Processes.user _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename _time as anydesk_first_seen | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as cred_time values(Filesystem.file_name) as cred_file values(Filesystem.process_name) as cred_proc values(Filesystem.file_path) as cred_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="Login Data" OR Filesystem.file_name="Cookies" OR Filesystem.file_name="Web Data" OR Filesystem.file_name="Local State") Filesystem.file_path="*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*" Filesystem.process_name!="chrome.exe" Filesystem.process_name!="msedge.exe" Filesystem.process_name!="GoogleUpdate.exe" by host Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename _time as cred_first_seen ] | where cred_first_seen >= anydesk_first_seen AND (cred_first_seen - anydesk_first_seen) <= 86400 | table host user anydesk_first_seen anydesk_cmd cred_first_seen cred_proc cred_file cred_path
ucs.kql:
// Evilnum hands-on-keyboard: portable AnyDesk + browser-credential SQLite read on the same host within 24h
let _window = 24h;
let _portable_paths = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Downloads\"]);
let _legit_browser_procs = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","brave.exe","firefox.exe","opera.exe","googleupdate.exe","msmpeng.exe","mssense.exe"]);
let _anydesk_launches =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName =~ "AnyDesk.exe" or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "AnyDesk.exe"
    | where FolderPath has_any (_portable_paths)
    | project AnyDeskTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              AnyDeskCmd = ProcessCommandLine, AnyDeskPath = FolderPath;
let _cred_reads =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    // Touching Chrome/Edge/Brave credential or cookie SQLite stores
    | where FolderPath has @"\User Data\"
         and FileName in~ ("Login Data","Cookies","Web Data","Local State","Login Data For Account")
    // Excluding the browsers themselves and known security agents
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_legit_browser_procs)
    | project CredTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              CredProc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              CredCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              CredFile = FileName,
              CredPath = FolderPath;
_anydesk_launches
| join kind=inner _cred_reads on DeviceId
| where CredTime between (AnyDeskTime .. AnyDeskTime + _window)
| project DeviceName, AccountName,
          AnyDeskTime, AnyDeskPath, AnyDeskCmd,
          CredTime, CredProc, CredCmd, CredFile, CredPath,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', CredTime, AnyDeskTime)
| order by AnyDeskTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Evilnum hands-on-keyboard — Sentinel port using SecurityEvent (4688). Note: file-event side requires MDE→Sentinel connector or ImFileEvent; if absent, the AnyDesk leg alone is still a useful hunting signal.
let _window = 24h;
let _portable_paths = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Downloads\"]);
let _legit_browser_procs = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","brave.exe","firefox.exe","opera.exe","googleupdate.exe"]);
let _anydesk_launches =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName endswith @"\AnyDesk.exe"
    | where NewProcessName has_any (_portable_paths)
    | project AnyDeskTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              AnyDeskCmd = CommandLine, AnyDeskPath = NewProcessName;
let _cred_reads =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where TargetFilePath has @"\User Data\"
        and TargetFileName in~ ("Login Data","Cookies","Web Data","Local State","Login Data For Account")
    | where tolower(ActingProcessName) !in (_legit_browser_procs)
    | project CredTime = TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname,
              CredProc = ActingProcessName,
              CredCmd  = ActingProcessCommandLine,
              CredFile = TargetFileName,
              CredPath = TargetFilePath;
_anydesk_launches
| join kind=inner _cred_reads on Computer
| where CredTime between (AnyDeskTime .. AnyDeskTime + _window)
| project Computer, Account,
          AnyDeskTime, AnyDeskPath, AnyDeskCmd,
          CredTime, CredProc, CredCmd, CredFile, CredPath,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', CredTime, AnyDeskTime)
| order by AnyDeskTime desc
ucs.rationale: Evilnum is the only well-documented financially motivated cluster that combines portable AnyDesk for live operator interaction with chrome credential/cookie theft against forex and crypto-trading employees (ESET 2020, Zscaler PyVil 2020, Proofpoint 'Jive Spy' overlap). Most ransomware crews use AnyDesk too — but they rarely chase Chrome's `Login Data` SQLite. Most infostealers grab Chrome creds —
